feature: Inventory and dependency graph: Terraform state / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on Terraform state cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for Terraform state: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover Terraform state, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for Terraform state.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for Terraform state.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for IaC Drift and Change Intelligence Terraform state inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: terraform-state_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Terraform state cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Terraform state; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Terraform state.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for Terraform state, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Terraform state.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for Terraform state.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing Terraform state data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling Terraform state inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for Terraform state.
acceptance_criteria: Given Terraform state data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Terraform state connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for Terraform plan: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for Terraform plan security posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for Terraform plan.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for Terraform plan.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for IaC Drift and Change Intelligence Terraform plan automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: terraform-plan_security_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Terraform plan security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Terraform plan; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Terraform plan.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Terraform plan recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Terraform plan.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for Terraform plan.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing Terraform plan data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling Terraform plan automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for Terraform plan.
acceptance_criteria: Given Terraform plan data exists, when the security automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Terraform plan connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for Pulumi stack: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize Pulumi stack evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for performance issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for Pulumi stack.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for Pulumi stack.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for IaC Drift and Change Intelligence Pulumi stack ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: pulumi-stack_performance_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Pulumi stack performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Pulumi stack; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Pulumi stack.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Pulumi stack recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Pulumi stack.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for Pulumi stack.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing Pulumi stack data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling Pulumi stack ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for Pulumi stack.
acceptance_criteria: Given Pulumi stack data exists, when the performance ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Pulumi stack connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for policy-as-code gate: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover policy-as-code gate, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the scalability pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for policy-as-code gate.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for policy-as-code gate.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for IaC Drift and Change Intelligence policy-as-code gate inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: policy-as-code-gate_scalability_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for policy-as-code gate scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for policy-as-code gate; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for policy-as-code gate.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for policy-as-code gate recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for policy-as-code gate.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for policy-as-code gate.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing policy-as-code gate data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling policy-as-code gate inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for policy-as-code gate.
acceptance_criteria: Given policy-as-code gate data exists, when the scalability inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: policy-as-code gate connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
